story: On July 13, 2026 the GVEA board is set to decide, in executive session, whether to buy a second ~$120M GE LM6000 gas turbine for its North Pole plant. News-Miner ties surging turbine demand to large data centers needing guaranteed power; the same AI-power question is arriving across Alaska as the Air Force offers ~4,700 acres on three bases for AI data centers, while GVEA members have just absorbed the largest fuel-charge increase in the co-op's history.
vote_outcome_status: NOT yet public. No July 13/14 report of the board decision found (News-Miner search 429; the vote is an executive-session item). Build the deck around the DECISION on the table, never an asserted approval/denial.

claims.text: GVEA's board is set to weigh buying a second LM6000 turbine in an executive session on July 13.
claims.verbatim: during an executive session on July 13
claims.attribution_note: Single primary source (News-Miner). Vote is in executive session; outcome not public.
claims.caveat: Frame as the decision on the table, not a result.

claims.text: A new LM6000 unit would cost about $120 million.
claims.verbatim: about $120 million for a new unit
claims.attribution_note: News-Miner reporting.

claims.text: News-Miner reports demand for the units has increased due to a surge in large data centers that require a guaranteed power supply.
claims.verbatim: Demand for the units has increased due to a surge in large data centers that require a guaranteed power supply
claims.attribution_note: News-Miner's characterization of the turbine MARKET, not GVEA's stated rationale and not a GVEA quote.
claims.caveat: HONESTY GUARDRAIL: never state the GVEA turbine is 'for data centers.' Always attribute: 'the News-Miner reports.'

claims.text: In April 2026 the board approved $50,000 to reserve an order spot for the generator.
claims.verbatim: approved $50,000 to reserve an order spot for the generator
claims.attribution_note: News-Miner reporting.

claims.text: The board approved a $25.6 million overhaul of its 1970s-era GT1 diesel generators.
claims.verbatim: approved a $25.6 million overhaul of its 1970s-era GT1 diesel generators
claims.attribution_note: News-Miner reporting.

claims.text: An LM6000 can generate between 45 and 58 megawatts of power on its own.
claims.verbatim: can generate between 45 megawatts and 58 megawatts of power
claims.attribution_note: News-Miner reporting.

claims.text: Paired with a steam turbine or battery storage the unit could produce between 76 and 152 megawatts.
claims.verbatim: could produce between 76 and 152 megawatts
claims.attribution_note: News-Miner reporting.

claims.text: Ordering and installing a new LM6000 could take at least three to five years.
claims.verbatim: Ordering and installing one could take at least three to five years
claims.attribution_note: News-Miner reporting.

claims.text: GVEA's president is Travis Million.
claims.verbatim: GVEA President Travis Million
claims.attribution_note: Titled 'President' by the source; KUAC calls him CEO. Use 'President' or 'CEO', both sourced.

claims.text: The North Pole plant installed a 47-megawatt naphtha-fired LM6000 in 2006 along with a 17-megawatt steam turbine.
claims.verbatim: The North Pole Power Plant installed an 47-megawatt naphtha-fired LM6000 in 2006 along with 17-megawatt steam turbine
claims.attribution_note: Single-sourced (News-Miner). The existing unit is naphtha-fired; a second unit would make a matched pair.

claims.text: The LM6000 is made by General Electric.
claims.verbatim: General Electric
claims.attribution_note: Source says 'General Electric' (the LM6000 line is now GE Vernova, but use the fetched wording).
claims.caveat: Do NOT print 'GE Vernova' as sourced. Prefer to just say 'LM6000' on slides.

claims.text: The Department of the Air Force is offering approximately 4,700 acres of underutilized land for potential AI data centers.
claims.verbatim: approximately 4,700 acres of underutilized land
claims.attribution_note: Primary source (DAF). Out of window (April) - CONTEXT.
claims.caveat: Loads are unbuilt/unbound; no lease awarded.

claims.text: Twelve parcels have been identified for potential data-center development across JBER, Eielson AFB and Clear Space Force Station.
claims.verbatim: Twelve parcels have been identified for potential development.
claims.attribution_note: Primary source (DAF). NOTE: Eielson and Clear are Interior bases; JBER is in Anchorage (NOT on GVEA's grid).
claims.caveat: Context; keep any grid linkage thematic, not a specific interconnect claim.

claims.text: The parcels would be offered under the Enhanced Use Lease model, which leases non-excess property to partners for long-term use.
claims.verbatim: Under the Enhanced Use Lease model, the service leases non-excess property to partners for long-term use.
claims.attribution_note: Primary source (DAF).

claims.text: The FY2027 NDAA provisions would require rigorous grid impact assessments before executing or renewing any Enhanced Use Lease for a data center.
claims.verbatim: rigorous grid impact assessments, requiring a formalized review of energy demands and commercial grid stability, before executing or renewing any Enhanced Use Lease (EUL)
claims.attribution_note: Primary (senator's release). FY2027 NDAA is a defense AUTHORIZATION, NOT yet law.

claims.text: The FY2027 NDAA would direct a strategy and roadmap to mitigate local community impacts during construction of private data centers on military installations.
claims.verbatim: to establish a comprehensive strategy and roadmap to mitigate local community impacts during the construction of private data centers on military installations
claims.attribution_note: Primary. Authorization, not law.

claims.text: According to Sullivan's office, the FY2027 defense bill authorizes about $2.6 billion for Alaska.
claims.verbatim: $2.6 billion in military construction and projects specifically for Alaska
claims.attribution_note: A senator's self-promoting release; one-sided framing.

claims.text: The Fairbanks North Star Borough is designated a 'Serious' PM2.5 nonattainment area under the 2006 standard.
claims.verbatim: PM-2.5 (2006) Fairbanks, AK ... Serious
claims.attribution_note: EPA Green Book. Air-quality context for added gas/diesel generation.
claims.caveat: Standing designation, not a July 2026 event; label as background.

claims.text: GVEA's fuel and purchased power charge rose 7.8 cents per kilowatt hour to 20.7 cents per kilowatt hour effective June 1, 2026, an increase of 61 percent over the prior quarter.
claims.verbatim: up 7.8 cents per kilowatt hour to 20.7 cents per kilowatt hour, an increase of 61% over last quarter
claims.attribution_note: Effective June 1 (recent backdrop, out of window). GVEA's primary release corroborates via search snippet.
claims.caveat: Label as recent backdrop; NOT caused by data centers.

claims.text: The average GVEA member's monthly bill rose about $45.74 under the June 1, 2026 fuel-charge change.
claims.verbatim: the average user would see a $45.74 increase in their electric bill
claims.attribution_note: Recent backdrop.

claims.text: The fuel GVEA buys for its power plants rose from about $2.50 a gallon in late February to $5.10 a gallon, more than double.
claims.verbatim: fuel prices were about $2.50 a gallon for what we were buying for our power plants. Fast-forward to today, it's $5.10 per gallon for that same fuel product
claims.attribution_note: Quote from CEO Travis Million. Recent backdrop.

claims.text: GVEA has been running its diesel units harder because it is not getting energy from Southcentral, per CEO Travis Million.
claims.verbatim: we're running those diesel units more to meet demand, because we're not getting any energy from Southcentral
claims.attribution_note: CEO quote; ties Interior generation to the Cook Inlet/Southcentral gas squeeze without needing No.4's dropped numbers.

claims.text: GVEA has seen higher electricity load across residential and industrial members than historically, per CEO Travis Million.
claims.verbatim: We did see higher load across the board. On the residential and on the industrial side, we're just using more electricity than what we had traditionally seen historically
claims.attribution_note: CEO's OWN words on load growth (general, not data centers). This is GVEA's own framing of demand growth, distinct from c3.
claims.caveat: General load growth; do NOT attribute to data centers.

claims.text: GVEA's June 1, 2026 fuel-charge increase was the largest in the co-op's history.
claims.verbatim: the most significant rate increase in GVEA's history
claims.attribution_note: Recent backdrop.

honesty_guardrails: Never say the GVEA turbine is 'for data centers.' c3 is News-Miner's characterization of the turbine MARKET, not GVEA's rationale. Always attribute 'the News-Miner reports.' GVEA's OWN framing (c23) is general 'higher load across the board.'
The July 13 vote is in executive session; outcome NOT public. Frame around the DECISION on the table, never an approval/denial.
Air Force EUL (April) and FY2027 NDAA (June) are OUT of window = CONTEXT. NDAA is authorization, NOT law: say 'would require' / 'if enacted.'
Grid linkage is THEMATIC/geographic, not a sourced interconnect. Eielson and Clear are Interior bases; JBER is in Anchorage (NOT GVEA's grid). Do not claim GVEA would serve all three.
Manufacturer is 'General Electric' per the source; prefer just 'LM6000' on slides.
PM2.5 'Serious' nonattainment is a standing designation, not a July event; background.
Fuel spike (c19-c24) is a recent BACKDROP effective June 1, NOT caused by data centers. It shows members already absorbed a record increase and load is already growing.
Keep MW, dollars, acres, gallons in their own units; no MW-to-data-center or capacity conversions.
GVEA is a member-owned cooperative; a $120M purchase is recouped through member rates (long-term debt on members).
dropped_unverifiable_this_run: STAK North Slope campus figures (Northern Journal/ADN URLs 404 to the fact-checker) - excluded; also No.1 territory.
RCA/Enstar Cook Inlet storage numbers (URL 404) - excluded; also No.4 territory. The Southcentral-gas squeeze survives only via CEO Million's c22 quote.
GVEA ~250,000 gal/day diesel; June 29 EUL deadline; RFLP mitigation language; specific 138kV interconnect recommendation - not on a fetched page; excluded.
